briefing.opening: Good morning. I will now begin the climate briefing for April 20, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis and Domestic and International Health Threats
sections.content: Health threats due to climate change are being severely reported both domestically and internationally. Domestically, wildfires and fine dust caused by climate change are proving fatal to kidney health, with the risk of kidney disease increasing threefold during heatwaves [2, 10]. Internationally, warnings have emerged that climate change could make outdoor activities difficult for asthma patients and further increase the risk of humid heatwaves [207, 208]. Furthermore, concerns are rising that climate change could lead to the collapse of modern agriculture, with wildfires that once ceased activity at night now burning continuously through the night. Reports also indicate a 5.1% decline in public awareness regarding the severity of climate change [211, 213, 214]. Editorials have even appeared, stating that this situation could lead to an unavoidable health crisis, emphasizing the urgency of diagnosing the climate crisis and seeking response measures [217, 54, 84].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Nationwide 'Climate Change Week' and the Spread of Carbon Neutrality Practices
sections.content: To commemorate 'Earth Day' and 'Climate Change Week,' which begin today, various events are being held nationwide to promote carbon neutrality practices [3, 8, 17, 18, 27, 29, 36, 55]. The Ministry of Climate, in cooperation with the UN, is hosting a Climate Change Week of unprecedented scale, using it as an opportunity to test its capacity to host a Climate Summit. Minister Kim Sung-hwan declared the 'Great Green Transition' as a path to growth for everyone [5, 43, 72, 76]. Various local governments, including Busan, Daejeon, Ulsan, Hwaseong, and Sejong, are expanding '10-minute lights-out for Earth' events, citizen-participatory carbon neutrality campaigns, and ecological education [3, 14, 29, 42, 44, 46, 60, 64, 69, 125, 126, 146, 154]. Public institutions, including KEPCO Nuclear Fuel, and various local communities are operating daily carbon neutrality practice programs such as the 'Computer OFF Challenge,' raising public awareness and encouraging action regarding climate crisis response [20, 93, 101, 106, 109, 113, 114, 130].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Energy Transition and Technological Innovation
sections.content: Both domestically and internationally, the movement towards renewable energy transition is accelerating, with increased efficiency through technological innovation drawing significant attention. Even amidst wartime, Spain's renewable energy sector is playing a stable role, contributing to energy security. Analysis also suggests that bundling renewable energy into a system can be 53% cheaper than nuclear power [158, 157]. Domestically, Minister Kim Sung-hwan of the Ministry of Climate expressed his ambition in Yeosu to make renewable energy transition a national growth strategy [181, 200]. Hanwha Q CELLS is leading advancements in solar technology, showcasing perovskite tandem cells at Korea's largest renewable energy exhibition [183, 185, 186, 188, 195, 197, 203]. Furthermore, the energy transition in the livestock industry is accelerating, with government support programs prioritizing and offering low-interest loans for the introduction of renewable energy in livestock farms and slaughterhouses [176, 180, 187, 198, 201]. Samsung SDI is also contributing to the transition to eco-friendly mobility by signing its first supply contract for electric vehicle batteries with Mercedes-Benz [204].

sections.title: ⚠️ Challenges and Dilemmas in Climate Response Policies
sections.content: Many challenges and dilemmas still exist in implementing policies to address the climate crisis. The Bank of Korea has been criticized for its lukewarm attitude towards climate change response for four years, having implemented only two of its assigned tasks, indicating an inadequate role for the institution [1]. Meanwhile, Samsung Electronics has requested an acceleration of the construction of an LNG power plant for its Pyeongtaek factory, illustrating the dilemma between achieving carbon neutrality and becoming an AI powerhouse [99, 177]. Nuclear power organizations are strongly opposing the government's renewable energy-centric policies, highlighting sharp disagreements over the direction of energy policy [160, 192]. Foreign media have expressed skepticism about the Lee Jae-myung administration's renewable energy transition policies, anticipating many obstacles in phasing out fossil fuels. There have also been calls to prioritize 'details' over 'packaging' in expanding renewable energy [161, 165]. Particularly, in the Seoul metropolitan area, where renewable energy supply is not smooth, claims have been made that semiconductor factory construction is impossible. The uncomfortable truth that solar power is discarded due to heating demand has also emerged, indicating the complexity of energy transition [156, 182].

sections.title: 🌍 International Community's Climate Change Discussions and Actions
sections.content: In the international community, diverse discussions and actions surrounding climate change are ongoing. At the 2026 UN Indigenous Peoples' Forum, issues of war, climate change, and artificial intelligence (AI) are expected to be major agenda items [205]. Additionally, the argument has been made that the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency's (EPA) repeal of its endangerment finding will not end climate change regulations [206]. On campuses, efforts are underway to spread climate change action and awareness through 'tUrn Week' events [210], and architects are inspiring students to design buildings prepared for climate change [215]. Furthermore, the role of civil society is also prominent, with one environmental activist winning a global environmental award for leading a landmark ruling against fossil fuel emissions through a fight against oil drilling in the UK [219].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlighted the severity of the climate crisis, global efforts towards energy transition, and the policy challenges encountered in that process. For a sustainable future, international cooperation and proactive domestic action will become even more crucial. This concludes today's climate briefing.
